seoTitle: Professional Floor Care Onboarding & Readiness | CARSI
metaDescription: CARSI's internal floor-care onboarding: WHS, PPE and SDS, surface identification, cleaning and sealing methods, machinery, hazard control and job evidence.
imageAlt: Floor-care technician operating a rotary scrubbing machine with wet-floor signage in an Australian school corridor.

og.title: Professional Floor Care Onboarding — CARSI Operational Readiness
og.description: CARSI Maintenance Company's internal floor-care induction — health and hygiene, WHS and chemical safety, surface identification, methods, machinery, site setup, conduct, and a final assessment.

faq.a: It is the internal induction for CARSI Maintenance Company floor-care technicians, supervisors and facilities staff who deliver cleaning, polishing, sealing, maintenance and hygiene services across schools, childcare, education, public buildings and high-traffic commercial sites.

faq.q: Does this course award IICRC CECs?
faq.a: No. It is an internal operational-readiness induction and does not award continuing education credits. It supports good judgement and does not replace the SDS/TDS, standards, or a site's own rules.

faq.q: What does the final assessment involve?
faq.a: A supervised, end-to-end standard job assessed against the checklists: vehicle and equipment readiness, site arrival and setup, surface identification and correct method, safe machine operation and hazard control, professional conduct and confidentiality, and close-out with evidence and handover. A pass requires at least 85% overall and 100% on every safety-critical and reputation-critical item.

WHS duties, the hierarchy of controls, PPE selection from the SDS, reading Safety Data Sheets, chemical labelling and storage, spill response, and electrical and trip safety, including RCD/safety-switch use and test-and-tag referencing AS/NZS 3760.

courseJsonLd.name: CARSI Maintenance Company Onboarding — Professional Floor Care & Operational Readiness
courseJsonLd.description: CARSI Maintenance Company's internal onboarding for floor-care technicians and supervisors, covering health and hygiene, WHS and chemical safety, surface identification, cleaning and sealing methods, machinery, site setup, conduct and a final practical assessment. This is an internal induction and does not award continuing education credits (CECs).
courseJsonLd.coursePrerequisites: None — internal induction for CARSI Maintenance Company floor-care staff.
